content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Find the number of integer sequences X1,X2,…,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1​,X2​,…,XN​ such that


0≤Xi<2390 \le X_i \lt 2390≤Xi​<239 for each valid iii
Xi≠XjX_i \neq X_jXi​=Xj​ for each valid i≠ji \neq ji=j
(X1⋅A1)+(X2⋅A2)+…+(XN⋅AN)(X_1 \cdot A_1) + (X_2 \cdot A_2) + \ldots + (X_N \cdot A_N)(X1​⋅A1​)+(X2​⋅A2​)+…+(XN​⋅AN​) is divisible by 239239239
Since the answer may be large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN (0 ≤ Ai < 239).
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer – the number of sequences X1,…,XN satisfying the conditions, modulo 998244353.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All 3 coefficients are non‑zero, so answer = P(239,3)/239 = (239·238·237)/239 = 56 406.

solution.approach: All Xi must be distinct residues modulo 239. The total number of injective assignments is P(239,N)=239·238·…·(239‑N+1). If every Ai is 0 (mod 239) the divisibility condition is always true, so the answer equals P(239,N). Otherwise at least one coefficient is non‑zero. For a fixed set of N distinct values the sum Σ Ai·Xi runs uniformly over the 239 residues when we permute the values, therefore exactly 1/239 of all injective assignments satisfy the divisibility condition. Hence answer = P(239,N) / 239 (mod 998244353). The division is performed by multiplying with the modular inverse of 239. The algorithm runs in O(N) time and O(1) extra space.
